agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby 2025 Annual Municipal Report presented to Council
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider the 2025 Annual Municipal Report and a shorter Highlights version at an upcoming meeting. This report is a yearly summary of the city's finances, services, and major projects from the past year. It is not a new law or bylaw, but it gives business owners a clear picture of how the city spent money and what priorities were set. The report is for information and discussion — no immediate rule changes are proposed.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1
2025 ANNUAL MUNICIPAL REPORT AND ANNUAL MUNICIPAL REPORT HIGHLIGHTS
Purpose: To present the 2025 Annual Municipal Report and 2025 Annual Municipal Report Highlights.

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby to replace Development Procedures Bylaw, update fees — effective July 1, 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on repealing the current Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw (2022) and replacing it with a new Development Procedures Bylaw (2026), effective July 1, 2026. The proposal also includes amendments to the Burnaby Building Bylaw (2016) and the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. If approved, these changes would update the rules and fees for development applications, building permits, and related processes in Burnaby, including new development permit types and formalized fee and refund practices.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — if you are planning a development or building project in Burnaby, the new rules and fees could affect your application.

key_dates.label: Proposed effective date — Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw 2026

agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.2
PROPOSED REPEAL AND REPLACEMENT OF BURNABY DEVELOPMENT PROCEDURES BYLAW AND RELATED AMENDMENTS TO THE BURNABY BUILDING BYLAW AND BURNABY CONSOLIDATED FEES AND CHARGES BYLAW
Purpose:
To seek Council approval to repeal
Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw, 2022
and replace it with the proposed
Development Procedures Bylaw, 2026
, to amend the
Burnaby
Building Bylaw, 2016
and
Burnaby
Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw.

attachments.title: Proposed Repeal and Replacement of Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw and Rela

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to apply for UBCM award on development approvals
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider endorsing an application for the 2026 UBCM Community Excellence Awards. This is based on the final report for Phase 3 of the Development Approvals Process (DAP) program. The DAP program aims to speed up and improve how the city handles development applications. If endorsed, the application would recognize Burnaby's work on making the development approval process more efficient. This is a proposed step — no decision has been made yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.3
DAP PROGRAM - PHASE 3 FINAL REPORT
Purpose: To provide an update on the activities and outcomes of the Development Approvals Process (DAP) program and seek endorsement to apply for the 2026 UBCM Community Excellence Awards.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract for mobile devices and data plans
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply and delivery of mobile devices, services, subscription plans, and other data services. If approved, this contract would set the terms for how the city purchases and manages mobile technology for its operations. The decision is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting and has not yet been finalized.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.4
CA - MOBILE DEVICE AND PLANS
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply and delivery of mobile devices, services, subscription plans, and other data services.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on aeration and overseeding contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for aeration and overseeding services. This means the city would hire a company to punch small holes in the soil (aeration) and spread grass seed (overseeding) on city-owned fields and parks. If approved, this contract would cover work on sports fields, parks, and other green spaces. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider the proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.5
CA - AERATION AND OVERSEEDING SERVICES
Purpose:
T
o obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for aeration and overseeding services.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract for Victory St. road upgrades from Patterson to Jubilee
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a contract for road upgrades on Victory Street, between Patterson Avenue and Jubilee Avenue. If approved, this would fund construction work to improve the road. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting and has not yet been finalized.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.6
CA - (CB-3233) VICTORY ST. - PATTERSON TO JUBILEE ROAD UPGRADES
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Victory St. – Patterson to Jubilee Road Upgrades project.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on sidewalk contract for five streets
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on awarding a contract for new sidewalk construction on Triumph, Carleton, Rosser, Gilmore, and Madison streets. This is a proposed decision — if approved, the city would hire a contractor to build the sidewalks. The project is part of Burnaby's ongoing work to improve pedestrian safety and walkability. Business owners near these streets should be aware that construction could affect access, parking, or foot traffic if the contract is awarded.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, prepare for possible construction disruptions on these five streets.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.7
CA - (CA-3299) TRIUMPH, CARLETON, ROSSER, GILMORE, AND MADISON SIDEWALK PACKAGE
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Triumph, Carleton, Rosser, Gilmore, and Madison Sidewalk Package project.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Contract change for Burnaby's Sidewalk Expansion Program
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change (a formal amendment to an existing agreement) for the Sidewalk Expansion Program. If approved, this change would alter the terms of the contract currently in place for building new sidewalks across the city. The decision is not final — Council will consider and vote on this proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.8
CC - (EB-8022) SIDEWALK EXPANSION PROGRAM
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) to the (EB-8022) Sidewalk Expansion Program.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider comment letters on Metro 2050 land-use amendments for Maple Ridge and Surrey
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider sending comment letters to the Metro Vancouver Regional District about two proposed changes to the regional growth strategy, Metro 2050. One change is proposed by the City of Maple Ridge for land near North 256 Street, and the other by the City of Surrey for the Hazelmere area. These amendments would allow different types of development in those areas. Council has not yet decided what to say in the letters — this is a chance for the city to weigh in before the regional district makes a final decision.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.9
COMMENT LETTERS ON PROPOSED METRO 2050 AMENDMENTS: CITY OF MAPLE RIDGE (NORTH 256 STREET) and SURREY (HAZELMERE)
Purpose:
To inform Council about two different site-specific Metro 2050 Amendments, one proposed by the City of Maple Ridge and the other by the City of Surrey, and to consider forwarding comment letters to the Metro Vancouver Regional District.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to review street width standards for new developments
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to consider a motion that would direct city staff to review Burnaby's street width standards for works and services. This means the city would look at how wide streets need to be for new developments, including roads, sidewalks, and utility corridors. The goal is to see if current rules are still appropriate or if they should be changed. This is a first step — no new rules have been passed yet. If approved, the review could lead to future bylaw changes that affect how streets are built in new neighbourhoods or redevelopment projects.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No immediate action needed — monitor council's decision on whether to launch the review.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.1
COTW - PROPOSED WORKS AND SERVICES REQUIREMENTS
Purpose: To direct staff to review the City's street width standards.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on list of consultant budget cuts and retained funding for 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposal that would require staff to prepare two separate lists: one showing all consultant budget cuts for 2026, including which departments and functions are affected, and another showing which consultants would keep their funding. This is not a final decision yet — Council will consider the motion at an upcoming meeting. If approved, the lists would help the city decide where to reduce spending on outside consultants next year.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this could affect businesses that contract with the city as consultants.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.2
FMC - 2026 CONSULTANT FUNDNG AND BUDGET REDUCTIONS
Purpose: To seek Council approval for staff to provide a comprehensive list of consultant budget cuts, including associated functions and departments, and a separate list of consultants retaining funding for 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Mountain Trail Management Plan update and direction
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a draft update to the Burnaby Mountain Trail Management Plan. This proposed plan would set new rules for trail use, including where mountain biking, hiking, and other activities are allowed. It could also change how trails are maintained and managed. The decision is not final yet — Council is being asked for direction on the draft plan at this meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision and any public consultation opportunities — this plan could affect access to trails near your business or property.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.3
PRCC - DRAFT BURNABY MOUNTAIN TRAIL MANAGEMENT PLAN
Purpose: To provide an update and seek Council direction on the Draft Burnaby Mountain Trail Management Plan.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider supporting Vancouver Roques Rugby's Bingham Cup 2028 bid
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a request from Vancouver Roques Rugby to support their bid to host the Bingham Cup in 2028. The Bingham Cup is a major international rugby tournament. Council's support would mean the City backs the bid, which could bring visitors and economic activity to the region. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.4
PRCC - VANCOUVER ROQUES RUGBY - BINGHAM CUP 2028 SUPPORT REQUEST
Purpose: To seek Council support for Vancouver Roques Rugby's bid to host the Bingham Cup in 2028.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Non-market housing project at 3961 and 3983 Norland Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed non-market housing project at 3961 and 3983 Norland Avenue. Non-market housing means homes that are rented or sold at below-market rates, often run by a non-profit or government agency, to make them more affordable. This is an update on the proposal — it has not been approved yet. Council will consider the project at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — this project could affect nearby property values and development plans.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.1
PROPOSED NON-MARKET HOUSING PROJECT AT 3961 AND 3983 NORLAND AVENUE
Purpose: To provide an update on the proposed non-market housing project at 3961 and 3983 Norland Avenue.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to review status update on Burnaby civic projects
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a status update on significant civic projects in Burnaby for April 2026. This report provides an overview of how major city-building projects are progressing. While no specific decisions are being made at this meeting, the update helps council and the public track timelines, budgets, and any delays. Business owners should watch for future council votes on individual projects that could affect construction, traffic, or local development.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Monitor future council meetings for specific project decisions that may impact your business operations or property.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.2
FMC - SU: SIGNIFICANT CIVIC PROJECTS - APRIL 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on significant civic projects.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to review status update on major Burnaby engineering projects
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a status update on major engineering projects for the period of December 2025 to April 2026. This is a report to keep council informed on the progress of large infrastructure projects. No decisions or new rules are being proposed at this time — it is an information update only.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.3
FMC - SU: MAJOR ENGINEERING PROJECTS - DECEMBER 2025 - APRIL 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on major Engineering Projects.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider status update on IT initiatives for early 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive a status update on the city's IT initiatives for the period of January to March 2026. This is a routine report to keep council informed about ongoing technology projects and their progress. No new rules or fees are proposed at this time.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.4
FMC - SU: IT INITIATIVES - JANUARY TO MARCH 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on the IT initiatives for January to March 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to consider new inclusion rules for public recreation
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider findings from a Langara College research project on Inclusion, Diversity, Equity, and Accessibility (IDEAs) in public recreation. The study looks at how to make Burnaby's recreation programs and facilities more welcoming and accessible for everyone, especially diverse communities. A proposed bylaw or policy change could follow, but no decision has been made yet. Business owners who run recreation programs or use city facilities should watch for upcoming council discussions that may affect how they operate.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for council's next steps — proposed changes could affect how you run recreation programs or use city facilities.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.5
PRCC - INCLUSION, DIVERSITY, EQUITY AND ACCESSIBILITY (IDEAS) IN PUBLIC RECREATION RESEARCH IN BURNABY
Purpose: To provide an update on the IDEAs in Public Recreation research project, led by Langara College, outlining its purpose, partnership model, and progress to date, while sharing emerging insights and demonstrating how the work supports improved access, inclusion, and participation in public recreation for Burnaby’s diverse communities.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Community Safety and Well-Being Plan update for 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a status update on the 2025 Community Safety and Well-Being Plan, which outlines local strategies to improve safety and quality of life. A proposed 2026 update to the plan is also up for discussion. This is not a final decision — Council is set to review the progress report and decide on next steps at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and consider providing feedback on the proposed 2026 plan update.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.6
PSC - SU: BURNABY COMMUNITY SAFETY AND WELL-BEING PLAN PROGRESS REPORT AND 2026 UPDATE
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on the 2025 Community Safety and Well-Being Plan.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Fire Department operations update to February 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive a status update on Burnaby Fire Department operations through February 2026. This is a routine report to keep council informed about the fire department's activities, response times, staffing, and equipment status. No new bylaws or fees are proposed at this time — it is an information-only item for council's awareness.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.7
PSC - SU: FIRE DEPARTMENT (FEBRUARY 2026)
Purpose: To provide Council with the Burnaby Fire Department's status update (SU) available to the end of February 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to review RCMP status report and policing performance plan
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a status update on RCMP policing operations for December 2025 to January 2026, along with the Annual Performance Plan (APP) quarterly report for October to December 2025. The APP is a plan that sets yearly goals for the police and tracks how well they are meeting those goals. This is a report for information only — no bylaw or rule changes are proposed at this time.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.8
PSC - SU: RCMP STATUS REPORT (DECEMBER 2025 - JANUARY 2026) AND ANNUAL PERFORMANCE PLAN QUARTERLY REPORT (OCTOBER - DECEMBER 2025)
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on policing operations covering December 2025 to January 2026 and the Annual Performance Plan (APP) quarterly report covering October to December 2025.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026 to replace 1965 version
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed new zoning bylaw that would replace the current 1965 version. The proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026 would repeal the old rules and introduce new Development Permit Area Guidelines and zoning district reassignments. This means property owners and businesses may face changes to what they can build, how they can use their land, and what permits they need. The decision has not been made yet — council will consider it at upcoming meetings.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date and attend to voice your concerns about how the new zoning rules could affect your property or business.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14815 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to repeal Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 and replace it with the proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026, including new Development Permit Area Guidelines and zoning district reassignment

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to update penalty rules for Zoning Bylaw 1965 violations
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would update the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw. Specifically, it would remove the old penalty rules for the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 and replace them with new penalty rules for the updated Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026. This means if you own property or run a business in Burnaby, the fines and enforcement process for zoning violations (like building without a permit or using a property for the wrong purpose) could change. The decision has not been made yet — council will consider this at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend or submit comments if you want to have a say on the new penalty rules.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.2
#14816 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 4, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (repeal Schedule A, Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 section and replace with Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to amend definitions of 'affordable housing' in ACC and DCC waiver bylaw
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would change the definitions of 'affordable housing' and 'Zoning Bylaw' in the Burnaby Waivers and Reductions of Amenity Cost Charges (ACC) and Development Cost Charges (DCC) Bylaw 2024. If approved, this amendment could affect which housing projects qualify for fee waivers or reductions. The changes are still under consideration and have not been finalized.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect eligibility for fee waivers on future housing projects.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.3
#14817 - Burnaby Waivers and Reductions of Amenity Cost Charges and Development Cost Charges Bylaw 2024, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Waivers and Reductions of ACC and DCC Bylaw 2024 (definitions of "affordable housing" and "Zoning Bylaw")

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw 2026 for redevelopment impacts
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw called the 'Tenant Protection Bylaw 2026'. This bylaw would create new rules to protect renters who are forced to move because their building is being torn down or redeveloped. If passed, the bylaw could require landlords to give tenants more notice, help with moving costs, or offer the right to return to a new unit at a similar rent. Council will consider this proposal at an upcoming meeting — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — this bylaw could affect your property if you plan to redevelop rental housing in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Construction & Development
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.4
#14821 - Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to establish protections for tenants impacted by redevelopment

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment to strengthen tenant protection enforcement
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw. This change would update how the city enforces tenant protection rules. If approved, it could mean faster or stronger penalties for landlords who break tenant protection laws. The goal is to give renters better protection under city rules. The decision has not been made yet — council will discuss and vote on it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and consider attending or submitting comments if you are a landlord or tenant in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.5
#14822 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 6, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (tenant protection)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to vote on mid-year fee and charge updates for 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would update the City of Burnaby's Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw with mid-year changes for 2026. If approved, this would adjust various fees the city charges for permits, licences, services, and other municipal costs. The exact fee changes are not listed in this agenda item, but business owners should watch for the council decision to see if their specific licence or permit fees will go up or down.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, check the updated fee schedule for any changes to your business licences or permits.
agenda_briefs.industries: Liquor, Cannabis, Construction & Development, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation, Spa & Wellness, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Grocery & Food Retail, Retail, Healthcare, Finance & Insurance, Technology, Natural Resources, Transportation & Logistics, Agriculture, Labour & Employment, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.6
#14823 - Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw (2026 mid-year amendments)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to finalize e-bike and scooter pilot rules
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on final adoption of a bylaw amendment that would allow e-bikes and electric kick scooters to be used on city streets as part of a pilot program. A bylaw is a local law passed by the city. If approved, this change to the Street and Traffic Bylaw would create new rules for where and how these devices can be ridden. The pilot program is designed to test how e-bikes and scooters fit into Burnaby's transportation network before any permanent rules are made. Council will consider this at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, new rules for e-bikes and scooters will take effect in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.1
#14811 - Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1964 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike and scooter pilot in parks
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of a bylaw that would allow e-bikes and electric kick scooters in certain Burnaby parks as part of a pilot program. The proposed amendment to the Parks Regulation Bylaw would create new rules for where and how these devices can be used. If approved, the pilot would run for a set period to test the impact on park users and safety. This is not yet law — council will decide at the next meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, the pilot rules will affect where you can ride e-bikes and scooters in Burnaby parks.

key_dates.label: Council vote on final adoption of e-bike and scooter pilot bylaw

agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.2
#14812 - Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw changes for e-bike and e-scooter pilot
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of a bylaw that would amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009. This change is part of a pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters. If approved, it would update how the city enforces rules for these devices — for example, how tickets or fines are issued. The decision has not been made yet; this is a proposed amendment up for a vote.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if passed, this could affect how e-bike and e-scooter rules are enforced in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.3
#14813 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026 sets property tax rates
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of the Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026. This proposed bylaw would set the tax rates for all taxable land and buildings in Burnaby for the 2026 tax year. It is based on sections 197 and 216 of the Community Charter, which give the city the authority to set property tax rates. If approved, this bylaw will determine how much property tax you will pay next year.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this will directly affect your property tax bill for 2026.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.4
#14814 - Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to establish tax rates upon all taxable land and improvements pursuant to sections 197 and 216 of the
Community Charter

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Remove office space, add 292 homes at 3900 Grand Promenade
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed bylaw amendment that would change an already-approved rezoning for 3900 Grand Promenade, 9850 Austin Road, and 9858/9898 Gatineau Place. The original plan (approved in September 2024) allowed two market strata residential towers (80 and 73 storeys) plus a 13-storey retail and office podium. The new proposal would remove the commercial office space and replace it with 292 additional market strata residential units. This is not yet approved — Council is set to vote on the amendment.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit written comments to Burnaby City Council before the vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.3.1
#14737 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 6, 2025 - REZ #24-23 (3900 Grand Promenade, 9850 Austin Road, and 9858 and 9898 Gatineau Place)
Purpose: to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands pursuant to REZ #18-32 (Bylaw No. 14447), which received Final Adoption on September 23, 2024, and included two market strata residential buildings of 80 and 73 storeys, respectively, inclusive of a 13-storey retail and office podium. The applicant is proposing to remove commercial office use and replace it with additional market strata residential use, which includes the proposal of 292 additional market strata units
